data.title: AMD Stock Rocks Up with Sass as AI and Quantum Trials Supercharge Outlook
data.description:
AMD stock (AMD) is seeing steady interest as the company enters what CEO Lisa Su has called a "defining year" for high-performance computing. AMD stock opened today at $207.32, currently supported by an influx of recent announcements that show AMD is ready to fight for more than just the silver medal in the AI race. While the market has seen some volatility lately, analysts at firms like DA Davidson and Benchmark have maintained their positive outlooks, with average price targets sitting around $288, suggesting there is still plenty of room for the stock to run as new products hit the shelves.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

AMD Targets Massive infrastructure Deals and 2026 Goals

The big story for AMD right now is its rapid expansion into global AI infrastructure. The company recently confirmed a partnership with Tata Consultancy Services to build a 200MW AI platform, but the ambitions go much further. AMD is currently on track to deploy up to 6 gigawatts of AI infrastructure in collaboration with OpenAI. This plan is built around the Helios platform, which can pack up to 72 high-power GPUs into a single server rack. This plug-and-play approach is designed to help big tech companies and governments build massive AI supercomputers without having to design the hardware from scratch.

AMD Focuses on New Chips and a Move into Quantum

Investors are keeping a close watch on the 2026 product roadmap, which is packed with major releases. Management recently confirmed that the MI440X GPU is launching this quarter to help businesses run AI models on their own private servers. Following that, the next-generation MI450 and the Venice EPYC processors are scheduled for later this year. Beyond traditional AI, AMD is also testing the waters of quantum computing. The company recently finished a trial with Comcast (CMCSA) that used quantum algorithms on AMD Instinct GPUs to make internet networks more reliable by automatically rerouting traffic during outages.

Financial Planning vs. Lack of Confidence

While there was some talk last week about CEO Lisa Su selling roughly $26.8 million in stock, most market experts view this as routine financial planning rather than a lack of confidence. In fact, professional money managers seem to be doing the opposite. Recent data shows that institutional ownership of AMD increased by over 3% last quarter, with firms like Long Walk Management and Vanguard adding significantly to their positions. These big buyers appear to be focused on the long-term potential of the MI400 series, which is expected to offer a 50% jump in performance over current models and could help AMD capture more of the $1 trillion AI chip market.

Is AMD a Buy or Sell Now?

Turning to Wall Street, AMD stock has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on 24 Buys and eight Holds assigned in the last three months. At $286.80, the average AMD price target implies a 41.2% upside potential.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Airbnb Stock (ABNB) Flies Higher as Global Travelers Get to Delay Holiday Payments
data.description:
Shares in hosting platform Airbnb (ABNB) jetted higher today after it rolled out its buy now pay later style service to help global travellers better afford their trips to the sun or the snow.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Reserve Now, Pay Later

The company said it was launching its "Reserve Now, Pay Later" feature, which lets users secure bookings without having to pay straight away – globally. It also allows users the chance to cancel their bookings if they have a change in their travel plans without losing money upfront.

The service was made available in the U.S. last year for domestic travelers and has already led to a growth in bookings. Indeed, at its recent Q4 earnings call, CFO Ellie Mertz said that it had also led to longer booking lead times and a mix shift towards larger entire homes, especially those with four or more bedrooms, contributing to an increase in average daily rate.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: Snap Stock Slips Despite Testing New Creator Subscriptions
data.description:
Snap (SNAP) stock was down on Tuesday after the Snapchat parent company announced a new source of revenue. The company has announced the introduction of Creator Subscriptions, which allow creators to create custom content for their most loyal subscribers.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Jim Shepherd, the Senior Director of Global Content Partnerships at Snap, said, "With Creator Subscriptions, Snapchatters will be able to subscribe to their favorite creators for exclusive content, including direct photos and videos, subscriber-only Stories, and prioritized replies. Creators will set their own price points and build recurring income streams rooted in their most loyal audiences."

Snap noted that Creator Subscriptions will start out small, with only some creators being offered the ability to test the feature. Snap also said that this new feature is a natural extension of the company's other subscription services, such as Snapchat+.

SNAP Stock Movement Today

Snap stock was down slightly on Tuesday, extending a 40.15% fall year-to-date. The stock has also decreased 56.25% over the past 12 months.

Trading activity today was muted, as some 92,000 shares of SNAP stock changed hands, compared to a three-month daily average trading volume of about 58 million units.

Unfortunately for Snap, today's stock movement suggests that investors aren't excited about the company's new Creator Subscriptions. Considering today's news is only a limited test of Creator Subscriptions, investors could warm up to it as it expands. What investors will really look for is the impact the feature will have on revenue and income after its full rollout.
data.author: William White

data.title: 'Not the Only Game in Town,' Says Top Investor About Netflix Stock
data.description:
Netflix (NASDAQ:NFLX) watchers have watched the company's share price dribble downward over the past few months. NFLX is down 18% this year alone.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Perhaps the biggest reason: the company's potential merger with Warner Bros. Discovery in a deal with an enterprise value of $82.7 billion.

That's a big price tag that stands to weigh down Netflix's balance sheet with debt, and folks are concerned. Some have even dubbed the company "Debtflix," as it lines up the financing required to pay for both the bride and the dowry.

Top investor Dan Caplinger believes that the market is paying too much attention to the pending Warner Bros. deal, and not looking hard enough at the company's additional efforts to drive growth.

"The merger is getting a lot of attention, but it's only one piece of the puzzle," emphasizes the 5-star investor, who is among the top 3% of stock pros covered by TipRanks. "There's more to Netflix's growth strategy than just acquiring content and production capacity from outside players."

Caplinger speaks about the company's quest to create original content, which has inspired legions of devoted fans around the world. He lists beloved titles such as Stranger Things, but also notes that Netflix is focused on plenty of tailored content that is geared for more specific audiences.

Other avenues for Netflix include additional mediums, including video games and podcasts. Caplinger also points out that the company is expanding into the physical space, citing recently launched Netflix Houses in the Dallas and Philadelphia metropolitan areas (which have Squid Game and Bridgerton immersive experiences).

When it comes to the merger, Caplinger believes that Netflix is banking on successfully utilizing the rich variety of content that Warner Bros. has in its vast library. In that sense, the investor predicts the company will take a page from the Disney playbook, which has created new content for fan-favorite franchises such as Star Wars and Marvel.

"Netflix hopes that it will be able to squeeze value from Warner Bros. legacy content that has been in the collective consciousness of entertainment fans for more than a century now," adds Caplinger.

Though he isn't planning on adding it to his portfolio at present, Caplinger will be keeping a close eye on this "increasingly compelling stock, particularly as it continues to see its share price fall from last year's highs." (To watch Dan Caplinger's track record, click here)

Wall Street is feeling pretty good about Netflix. With 27 Buys, 9 Holds, and 1 Sell, NFLX enjoys a Moderate Buy consensus rating. Its 12-month average price target of $114.81 points to an upside just shy of 50%. (See NFLX stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: 'Once in a Blue Moon,' Says Top Investor About Amazon Stock
data.description:
Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) hasn't been on a roll recently, and its latest quarterly report didn't exactly improve its standing. In fact, it only seemed to make things worse.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

It wasn't as if the company failed to deliver growth, as is often the case when disappointment reigns. In fact, its worldwide revenue of $213.4 billion was up 14% year-over-year. Moreover, AWS, an increasingly important growth-driver for the company, enjoyed its fastest spate of growth in over three years.

When it comes to profits, Amazon had a slight miss on EPS, coming in at $1.95 instead of the expected $1.97. Part of this was due to $2.4 billion in special charges, including severance payments and the resolution of a tax dispute.

Was that enough to push AMZN down by 10% over the past few weeks? Clearly, there was something else afoot. That would be the jaw-dropping $200 billion in capex the company plans to spend in the current year.

The figure jolted the market and sent some investors heading for the exits. Top investor Will Healy thinks the current dip represents a rare buying window.

"Across its nearly 29-year trading history, one might struggle to find a time when Amazon stock flirted with value stock territory," explains the 5-star investor, who is among the top 3% of stock pros covered by TipRanks.

Healy acknowledges the souring sentiment that stemmed from the massive $200 billion capex spend, which follows the $132 billion Amazon shelled out in 2025. And that's made investors worried.

And therein lies the opportunity, according to Healy, as the post-earnings selloff has pushed AMZN into a price-to-earnings ratio in the twenties.

"This is quite a turnabout for a stock that sold at more than 50 times earnings (and sometimes at a P/E ratio of over 100) in recent years," he adds.

Healy points out that the company has disclosed a $244 billion backlog for AWS and AI, hinting at no shortage of future revenues on the horizon. That makes him bullish about what comes next.

"Amazon stock could be in for a massive comeback as it monetizes more of its fast-growing AI and cloud businesses," concludes Healy. (To watch Will Healy's track record, click here)

Wall Street seems to agree with this take as well. With 41 Buys and 3 Holds, AMZN enjoys a Strong Buy consensus rating. Its 12-month average price target of $282.14 points to gains north of 40% in the year ahead. (See AMZN stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: Standard Chartered Slashes XRP Price Forecast by 65% as Bank Says 'We Expect Further Declines Near-Term'
data.description:
XRP (XRP-USD) investors are facing a worrisome reality check today after Standard Chartered (SCBFF), the 172-year-old British banking giant, officially walked back its most aggressive price predictions. In a new note to investors, the bank's digital assets team revealed it is slashing its end-of-2026 target for XRP by a staggering 65%. While the bank previously told clients to expect an $8 price tag, it now believes the token is more likely to land near $2.80.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

XRP Faces a Challenging Market Rout

The decision to lower the target comes after what analysts are calling a "brutal" start to February for the entire crypto sector. Over the last month, the broader market has seen trillions of dollars in value evaporate, and XRP even briefly dipped to a 15-month low of $1.16 before its recent small recovery.

Geoffrey Kendrick, the head of digital assets research at Standard Chartered, didn't sugarcoat the situation. In his report, he wrote that "Recent price action for digital assets has been challenging, to say the least." He further warned that we might not be at the bottom yet, stating, "We expect further declines near-term and we lower our forecasts across the asset class."

Why the Bank Changed its Mind

Standard Chartered pointed to a few specific reasons for its sudden change in heart:

  • ETF Outflows: The bank noted a significant drop in the amount of money flowing into crypto ETFs. Since the market peaked in October 2025, investors have been pulling cash out rather than putting it in.
  • Macro Pressure: With high interest rates and mixed economic data from the U.S., many big investors are choosing to play it safe with cash or bonds instead of risky assets like XRP.
  • Paper Losses: Many people who bought into crypto ETFs recently did so at much higher prices (around $90,000 for Bitcoin). Because they are currently losing money, they are more likely to sell out of fear than buy more during the dip.

It's Not Just an XRP Problem

The massive 65% cut to the XRP forecast was part of a broader move by Standard Chartered to reset expectations for the entire crypto market. XRP was not the only asset to see its future price slashed, as the bank also lowered its end-of-year predictions for the other major players. Bitcoin (BTC-USD), which previously had a target of $150,000, was reduced to $100,000. Ethereum saw its target nearly halved from $7,500 down to $4,000, while Solana (SOL-USD) was adjusted from $250 to just $135. These changes show that the bank sees a general cooling off for the whole digital asset class, not just a specific issue with Ripple or XRP.

At the time of writing, XRP is sitting at $1.4554.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Ireland Opens GDPR Investigation Into Elon Musk's X Over Grok Deepfakes: What It Means for AI Stocks
data.description:
Ireland's data watchdog has opened a large probe into Elon Musk's social media firm X over AI-made sexual images. The regulator said it will review how user data was used in the creation and posting of "potentially harmful" sexual images by the Grok chatbot.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Grok is built by xAI, Musk's AI firm, and is built into the X feed. The probe will test if X met its core duties under EU data law. That law, known as GDPR, sets strict rules on how firms use and store user data.

Graham Doyle, deputy chief at the Irish Data Protection Commission, said the agency has begun a "large-scale inquiry" into X. He added that the review will look at the firm's "compliance with some of their fundamental obligations under the GDPR."

The case follows media reports that users were able to prompt Grok to make sexual images of real people, in some cases said to include minors. The regulatory body said it had been in touch with X since those reports came out.

Rising Legal Risk for Musk's Tech Empire

This move adds to rising legal risk for Musk's tech group. France raided X's offices this month as part of a wider probe into AI-made abusive content. The UK data office also said it has "serious concerns" about Grok's use of personal data and its "potential to produce harmful sexualised image and video content."

In the meantime, EU officials are also probing xAI under the Digital Services Act, which forces large tech firms to curb illegal and harmful posts.

X has said it removes child abuse material and non-consent nude content. In a post after the French raids, the firm called the claims "baseless" and said it "categorically denies any wrongdoing."

As things stand, the issue seems to be less about content and more about risk. GDPR fines can reach up to 4% of global sales. That can add real cost for firms tied to Musk, even if X itself is private.

Moreover, the case shows that EU leaders are ready to test AI tools that sit inside social feeds. That may shape how other tech firms, such as Meta (META) and Alphabet (GOOGL), build and guard their own AI bots.

We used TipRanks' Comparison Tool to compare notable publicly traded companies that employ chatbots similar to ChatGPT, Grok, and Claude. It's an excellent tool to gain a broader perspective on each stock and the AI industry as a whole.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: This Is Why Gaxosai Stock (GXAI) Rocketed Today
data.description:
Gaxosai (GXAI) stock underwent a massive rally on Tuesday after the artificial intelligence (AI) applications development company announced that it had finalized a deal with BytePlus. BytePlus is the enterprise technology division of ByteDance, which is the Chinese company behind the Seedance AI video generator and TikTok.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Gaxosai revealed that it has reached an agreement with BytePlus for preferred pricing and significant discounts on the company's AI video generation models. This will allow Gaxosai to offer these models to its consumers through Art-Gen, which is its flagship AI image and video creation platform. The company has also been granted early access to new models from ByteDance.

Vadim Mats, CEO of Gaxosai, said, "We are thrilled to deepen our relationship with BytePlus and leverage ByteDance's world-class AI video technology. This partnership enhances our ability to deliver unparalleled creative power to users worldwide while driving stronger economics for our business. It is a significant step toward making Gaxos the go-to AI platform for both creators and enterprises."

Gaxosai Stock Movement Today

Gaxosai stock was up 60% in pre-market trading on Tuesday, following a 1.94% rally on Friday. The shares have decreased 1.97% year-to-date and 35.58% over the past 12 months.

With today's news came heavy trading of GXAI stock, as some 45 million shares changed hands. For comparison, the stock's three-month daily average trading volume is about 427,000 units.
data.author: William White
